title: Property Transfer Tax (Amendment) Act, 2024

sections.title: Section 2 of the principal Act is amended by the— Amendment
sections.body:
of section 2
(a) deletion of the definition of “intellectual property” and the
substitution therefor of the following definition:
“intellectual property” includes a—
(a) patent granted for an invention under the Act No. 40
Patents Act, 2016; of 2016
(b) design registered under the Industrial Designs Act No.22 of
Act, 2016; 2016
(c) trade mark registered under the Trade Marks Act No. 11 of
Act, 2023; and 2023
(d) copyright in a work registered under the Copy Cap. 406
Right and Performance Rights Act; and
(b) insertion of the following new definitions in the appropriate
places in alphabetical order:
Singles copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,
P. O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka. Price K4.00 each
402 No. 27 of 2024 Property Transfer Tax (Amendment)
“building society” has the meaning
Cap. 412 assigned to the words in the Building
Societies Act; and
“money-lender” has the meaning assigned
Cap. 398 to the words in the Money-Lenders
Act;.

sections.title: Section 4 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion of
sections.body:
Amendment
of section 4 subsection (2) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(2) The rate of tax is—
(a) ten percent of the realised value in respect of
a mining right for a mining licence;
(b) eight percent of the realised value in respect
of a mining right for an exploration licence;
(c) ten percent of the realised value in respect of
a mineral processing licence;
(d) eight percent of the realised value in respect
of land;
(e) eight percent of the realised value in respect
of shares; and
(f) eight percent of the realised value in respect of
intellectual property.
Amendment 4. Section 5 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion of
of section 5 subsection (5A) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(5A) Where a financial service provider, building society
or money-lender transfers a foreclosed property, the realised
value of the foreclosed property shall be the actual price, if
any, received by the financial service provider, building society
or money-lender.
